title: Lymphatic drainage
body_html:
Remember the basics
  • superficial lymphatic vessels drain the skin and follow venous drainage
  • superficial lymphatic vessels + drainage of internal organs → deep lymphatic vessels that follow the arteries
  • → lymph nodes → lymph trunks which unite to form either the right lymphatic duct or the thoracic duct
  • right lymphatic duct drains the right side of the head and right arm
  • thoracic duct drains everything else
  • both lymph ducts drain into the venous system

Primary lymph node drainage siteArea
Superficial inguinal lymph nodesAnal canal below pectinate line
Perineum
Skin of the thigh

Lower part of rectum
Pelvic structures, including cervix and inferior part of uterus
Superior mesenteric lymph nodesDuodenum
Jejunum
Inferior mesenteric lymph nodesDescending colon
Sigmoid colon
Upper part of rectum
Coeliac lymph nodesStomach
